blocks.body: Unless / Provided / As long as give you alternatives to if and help you express condition more precisely.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"Unless, provided, and as long as often replace if, but they do not mean exactly the same thing. They add the ideas of if not, only if, or while the condition is true.","rules":[{"label":"UNLESS","color":"emerald","text":"Unless usually means if not.","example":"Unless you hurry, you'll miss the bus."},{"label":"PROVIDED","color":"blue","text":"Provided or provided that makes the condition more explicit and restrictive.","example":"You can borrow the car provided that you fill the tank."},{"label":"AS LONG AS","color":"amber","text":"As long as shows that the result is possible only under a certain condition.","example":"You can stay here as long as you are quiet."}]}

{"title":"1. Core meanings","intro":"These linkers are similar, but each has its own shade of meaning:","rows":[{"en":"unless","ua":"if not","note":"Unless it rains, we'll go out."},{"en":"provided / provided that","ua":"only if, on condition that","note":"Provided that you study, you'll improve."},{"en":"as long as","ua":"if, so long as the condition continues","note":"You can join us as long as you are ready by six."},{"en":"if","ua":"neutral condition","note":"If you need help, call me."}],"warning":"⚠ After unless, do not add another negative. unless you don't... is usually wrong."}

{"title":"2. How to use them naturally","sections":[{"label":"Unless = if not","color":"emerald","description":"Use unless when you want a shorter way to say if not.","examples":[{"en":"Unless you book today, you won't get the discount.","ua":"Unless you book today, you won't get the discount."},{"en":"We can't start unless everyone is here.","ua":"We can't start unless everyone is here."}]},{"label":"Provided / as long as","color":"blue","description":"These forms sound stronger than plain if and highlight a permission condition.","examples":[{"en":"You can work from home provided that your internet is stable.","ua":"You can work from home provided that your internet is stable."},{"en":"I'll support the plan as long as the budget stays realistic.","ua":"I'll support the plan as long as the budget stays realistic."}]},{"label":"What not to confuse","color":"amber","description":"As long as can express both condition and duration. Context decides which meaning is active.","examples":[{"en":"You can stay as long as you like.","ua":"You can stay as long as you like."},{"en":"As long as the door is locked, the alarm stays off.","ua":"As long as the door is locked, the alarm stays off."}],"note":"In formal English, provided that often sounds more precise than plain if."}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using a double negative with unless.","wrong":"Unless you don't hurry, you'll be late.","right":"Unless you hurry, you'll be late.","hint":"Unless already contains the meaning of if not."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Confusing condition with precaution.","wrong":"Take an umbrella unless it rains.","right":"Take an umbrella in case it rains.","hint":"In case expresses precaution, not an entry condition."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using neutral if when you need a stronger restriction.","wrong":"You can use my laptop if you return it tonight.","right":"You can use my laptop provided that you return it tonight.","hint":"Provided that better expresses a stricter condition."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick rule","items":["Unless = if not.","Provided and as long as make the condition sound stronger or clearer.","Do not use an extra negative after unless.","If the meaning is precaution rather than condition, you probably need in case."]}
